sceneBadge.title: What this window is editing (level/asset) — double-click another item in the Assets panel to switch

viewportHints.closeTitle: Close (click the ? at the bottom-right to reopen)
viewportHints.row2: W / E / R move·rotate·scale · F focus selection · trackpad Alt+Left orbit · ⇧Alt pan

detachedPanel.redockTitle: Dock back to the main window

history.waitingForViewport: Waiting for Edit viewport… Open the "Edit" panel and every operation will show here in real time.

inspector.batchNote: Editing the shared properties below applies to all selected at once (single undo step).
inspector.noCommonComponents: Selected entities have no components in common.

launcher.title: Launcher
launcher.editingHint: Double-click another level/asset in the Assets panel to switch (or open in a new window)
launcher.campaignOption: Global (main) — full campaign, starting from level 1
launcher.configHint: Writes play-config.json (local launcher state, not committed); read on ▶ Play startup.

material.selectEntityHint: Select an entity to edit its material.
material.noAssetTitle: No asset referenced — using inline PBR
material.hint: Tip: right-click “Assign to selected entity” in the Assets panel to set the material asset GUID.

mesh.empty: Select a mesh asset, or an entity with a Mesh component. (For ‘whole’ imports, expand the scene first to select a mesh inside.)
mesh.uvChannelsTitle: Best-effort from vertex attributes; precise per-channel count is planned.
mesh.lodsTitle: The current engine has no LOD chain — meshes are single-LOD.
mesh.estSizeTitle: Host-side geometry bytes (vertex + index buffers); not the GPU resource size.
mesh.materialsNote: Per-section material binding lives on the instance (engine MeshRenderer).
mesh.hint: Read-only preview. Right-click an asset → “Assign to selected entity” to apply.
mesh.entityHint: Double-click a mesh asset in the Content Browser to see its geometry stats.

matgraph.empty: Select an entity to edit its material graph.
matgraph.outputPin: Output — click, then click an input
matgraph.hint: Click an output pin → then click an input pin to wire; click a connected input pin to disconnect. Graph changes apply to the Material immediately.

timeline.waitingViewport: Waiting for the Edit viewport… open the "Edit" panel, then select an entity to edit its animation.
timeline.selectEntity: Select an entity to edit its animation.
timeline.captureKey: Capture the current Transform as a keyframe at the playhead
timeline.removeKey: Delete the keyframe at the playhead
timeline.hint: Move the entity to a frame → press "● K" to capture a keyframe; frames are interpolated automatically.
timeline.keyTooltip: t={{t}}s v={{v}} ({{interp}}) — click to jump to this frame

assets.newLevelPrompt: New level id (lowercase letters/digits/hyphens, e.g. level3; i.e. file name scenes/<id>.pack.json):
assets.newLevelFailed: Failed to create level: id already exists / disk write failed
assets.menuDuplicateScene: ⧉ Duplicate current scene as new level…
assets.importSceneWarning:
{{warning}}

Confirm importing as a “single reference entity”?
assets.importRefDone:
Imported “{{name}}” ({{totalNodes}} nodes · {{meshCount}} mesh).

The real geometry is loaded asynchronously by the runtime glTF loader — you'll see a placeholder box first, replaced by the real model after a few seconds (works in both editor + Play). Editing commits may lag slightly when a large scene has many nodes.
assets.levelRowTitle:
{{pack}}
Double-click to open this level in this window
assets.assetRowTitle:
{{pack}}
Double-click to open this asset in the editor
assets.materialGroupTitle: Materials · double-click to apply to selected entity
assets.emptyPacks: No *.pack.json yet — import a GLB then click “Process” to generate
assets.emptyFiles: No imported files yet — use the “Import” button in the editor toolbar
assets.processTitle: Generate meta.json + sub-asset GUIDs; required before adding to a scene
assets.addToSceneTitle: Add a reference entity for this GLB at the current scene root
